links.quote: Platform defect — fixed 6 Dec 2024

links.rationale: D-005 is a post-implementation defect and is irrelevant to whether the design, build or implementation was delivered on time.

links.quote: your design, build and implementation of the inventory and EPOS platform under the above Agreement

links.rationale: Post-go-live failures relate to the Platform's quality and reliability after implementation, not to whether implementation itself was delayed.

links.rationale: This gives the actual UAT window but, without a contractual milestone date to compare it against, it neither supports nor contradicts a delay allegation.

proposition.text: The Defendant's conduct constituted a breach of contract.
proposition.quote: damages for breach of contract

links.rationale: Meridian's acceptance of TechFlow's conduct as terminating the MSA with immediate effect is consistent with a repudiatory breach, directly supporting the proposition that the Defendant's conduct constituted a breach of contract.
links.quote: Our client accepts your conduct as terminating the MSA with immediate effect

links.rationale: The total contract value establishes the scale of the commercial relationship but does not itself indicate whether a breach occurred.
links.quote: The total contract value was £2.4 million.

links.rationale: The exclusion clause limits the Defendant's liability for certain losses but does not address whether a breach occurred in the first place.
links.quote: the Supplier shall not be liable to the Customer, whether in contract, tort (including negligence) or otherwise, for any loss of profit, loss of revenue, loss of anticipated savings, or any indirect or consequential loss

links.rationale: Establishing Priya Nair's responsibility for contractual administration provides foundational context but does not address whether a breach took place.
links.quote: I was responsible for the contractual and commercial administration of the TechFlow contract.

links.rationale: The contract value figure corroborates the commercial relationship but has no bearing on whether a breach of contract occurred.
links.quote: Total (excl. VAT) |  | 2,400,000

proposition.text: The Defendant's conduct constituted negligence.
proposition.quote: damages for breach of contract and/or negligence

links.rationale: The carve-out preserving negligence claims from the limitation clause shows the parties contemplated negligence as a live liability, but it does not itself establish that the Defendant's conduct was negligent.
links.quote: Nothing in this Agreement limits liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, for fraud, or for any other liability that cannot lawfully be excluded

links.rationale: The exclusion clause addresses the financial consequences of tortious liability (including negligence) rather than whether negligence actually occurred, so it does not move the proposition either way.
links.quote: the Supplier shall not be liable to the Customer, whether in contract, tort (including negligence) or otherwise, for any loss of profit, loss of revenue, loss of anticipated savings, or any indirect or consequential loss

links.rationale: The acceptance of the Defendant's conduct as repudiating the MSA implies the conduct was seriously wrongful, which is tangentially supportive of a finding of negligence, though the legal concepts are distinct.
links.quote: Our client accepts your conduct as terminating the MSA with immediate effect

proposition.text: The Defendant's conduct constituted misrepresentation.
proposition.quote: damages for breach of contract and/or negligence and/or misrepresentation

links.rationale: The letter addresses acceptance of conduct as a basis for contractual termination (consistent with repudiatory breach), but makes no reference to false statements or misrepresentation, so it neither advances nor undermines the misrepresentation proposition.
links.quote: Our client accepts your conduct as terminating the MSA with immediate effect

proposition.text: The Claimant is entitled to interest pursuant to section 35A of the Senior Courts Act 1981.
proposition.quote: interest pursuant to section 35A of the Senior Courts Act 1981

links.rationale: Evidence about the technical cause of the outage is irrelevant to the statutory interest entitlement under s.35A.
links.quote: the cause was a loss of wide-area network connectivity at the Claimant's data-centre provider (Northgate Telecom)

links.rationale: Greenhalgh's methodology for assessing quantum does not bear on whether the court has jurisdiction or discretion to award interest under s.35A.
links.quote: I have analysed the Claimant's management accounts, EPOS sales data and prior-year comparatives for the November–December 2024 period.

links.rationale: This simply identifies Dr Whitfield's instructing solicitors and has no bearing on the Claimant's entitlement to statutory interest.
links.quote: I am instructed by Caldwell Pryce LLP, solicitors for the Claimant

links.rationale: The fact of the flooding is background context about the underlying loss event and does not address the Claimant's entitlement to interest under s.35A.
links.quote: the flooding of the Claimant's Lutterworth distribution centre in early December 2024

links.rationale: This identifies Greenhalgh's instructions on quantum and does not speak to whether interest is available under s.35A.
links.quote: I am instructed by Caldwell Pryce LLP to give my independent opinion on the quantum of the Claimant's pleaded losses

links.rationale: Corroboration of the wasted-expenditure quantum goes to the principal sum recoverable but does not address the separate question of interest entitlement.
links.quote: The pleaded wasted expenditure of £1,800,000 is consistent with the Claimant's accounting records and the sums invoiced and paid under the Master Services Agreement.

links.rationale: The pleaded wasted expenditure figure establishes a money sum in principle but does not directly address the legal basis for awarding interest upon it.
links.quote: wasted expenditure of £1,800,000

links.rationale: The pleaded loss of profit figure similarly establishes a money claim but says nothing about entitlement to statutory interest under s.35A.
links.quote: loss of profit of £4,200,000

links.rationale: The sector-wide retail downturn potentially undermines loss-of-profit quantum but does not speak to the binary question of whether s.35A interest is available at all.
links.quote: a sector-wide downturn in retail trading in the relevant quarter, evident in the Claimant's own like-for-like data from October 2024 (i.e. before go-live)

links.rationale: Submission to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales satisfies the jurisdictional prerequisite for s.35A of the Senior Courts Act 1981 to operate.
links.quote: the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales

proposition.text: The amount claimed is approximately £6,000,000.

links.rationale: A contractual liability cap of approximately £1,800,000 would limit recovery to less than a third of the £6,000,000 claimed, directly undermining the proposition.
